reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: All gates passed. Gate 2 (Trust Boundary): Property explicitly requires KZG verification on semi-trusted Engine API boundary; compromised EL is within attack model for semi-trusted components per property type 'assumption'. Gate 3 (Code Verification): Code reading is factually accurate - ELMemPoolRequirements is empty slice (verification/blob.go:77), GetBlobsV2 path uses NewVerifiedRODataColumn bypassing verification (engine_client.go:714). Gate 4 (Exploitability): Requires compromised EL (semi-trusted component), which is conditional per trust_assumptions but in-scope per property definition. Gate 5 (Spec Cross-Reference): 01e entry PROP-57888860-asm-003 in outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W3B4_1771748827.json requires 'verify_blob_kzg_proof(blob, commitment) == true before propagation' - code does not comply. Attack path is conditional on compromised EL rather than direct P2P exploitation; CONFIRMED_POTENTIAL reflects this uncertainty. An attacker with control over a compromised EL can inject blobs with invalid KZG proofs via GetBlobsV2, causing CL to propagate unverified blob data to network.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Engine API blob data is semi-trusted; blobs from EL must still pass KZG commitment verification.
